steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank ends the first clause of the sentence: "...photography by women in the 1920s through the [1950s ___]." What follows the blank is a second independent clause: "given the collection's breadth of more than 120 photos, its efforts were largely successful." Two independent clauses must be joined with a comma + coordinating conjunction, a semicolon, or a period — not left as a run-on.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "1950s": No punctuation at all between the two independent clauses — creates a run-on sentence. ✗
  • "1950s, and": A comma followed by the coordinating conjunction "and" correctly joins the two independent clauses. ✓
  • "1950s,": A comma alone cannot join two independent clauses — that is a comma splice. ✗
  • "1950s and": "And" without a preceding comma cannot join two independent clauses. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
